content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Closed the three pending lifecycle gaps. In src/cli/run.rs, forced-stop now snapshots descendant backend processes before TERM escalation and SIGKILLs those tracked descendants even if the orchestrator exits before the final kill step, so orphaned backend children no longer survive that race. In src/contexts/automation_runtime/cli_writer_lease.rs, resume/stop recovery can now detect and finish detached stale lease cleanup after the writer lock was already released, including clearing preserved daemon task lease references instead of letting partial-cleanup residue become invisible on the next retry. In src/cli/run.rs, non-authoritative legacy run.pid records are no longer treated as live from raw PID existence alone; status/stop now fall back to stale recovery unless a fresh lease proves the legacy run is still active, which avoids PID-reuse false positives. Added targeted regressions plus helper coverage for detached cleanup completion, orphan-child SIGKILL after parent exit, stale pid-only status, and stale stop recovery.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Patched run-stop escalation in src/cli/run.rs to capture descendant process identity before TERM, then SIGKILL tracked descendants after timeout so backend subprocess cleanup survives parent exit races.
2. [Completed] Extended stale lease recovery in src/contexts/automation_runtime/cli_writer_lease.rs so detached project lease residue can be discovered and cleaned after writer-lock loss, including finishing daemon partial-cleanup state and tolerating missing canonical lease files as absent records.
3. [Completed] Updated running liveness classification in src/cli/run.rs so pid-only legacy records without authoritative process identity no longer block stale recovery, while fresh lease-backed mixed-version runs still remain active.
4. [Completed] Added and updated regressions in src/cli/run.rs, src/contexts/automation_runtime/cli_writer_lease.rs, and tests/cli.rs, then reran formatting, targeted lifecycle tests, the full cargo test suite, and clippy.

## Validation Evidence

- cargo fmt --check passed.
- cargo test --test cli run_status_reports_stale_for_live_pid_without_proc_start_ticks_when_no_lease_exists -- --exact passed.
- cargo test --test cli run_stop_recovers_live_pid_record_without_proc_start_ticks_as_stale -- --exact passed.
- cargo test detached_project_writer_owner_finishes_partial_cleanup_after_lock_loss passed.
- cargo test survives_parent_exit passed, including cli::run::tests::kill_tracked_descendant_processes_survives_parent_exit.
- cargo test --test cli run_stop_sigkill_terminates_backend_subprocess_group -- --exact passed.
- cargo test --test cli run_stop_sigkill_terminates_same_process_group_backend_descendant -- --exact passed.
- cargo test passed.
- cargo clippy -- -D warnings failed only on pre-existing repository-wide lint findings outside this lifecycle patch.

## Outstanding Risks

- cargo clippy -- -D warnings still fails on pre-existing repository-wide lint debt outside this change set, with current findings in src/adapters/fs.rs, src/cli/project.rs, src/contexts/agent_execution/diagnostics.rs, src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs, src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs, src/contexts/requirements_drafting/service.rs, src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs, and src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs.
- The worktree already had unrelated live-state changes under .ralph-burning/projects/rlm/; those files were left untouched.
